D10 Imaging L3 Apply Reference

Spot · Center-Weighted · Evaluative Metering

three metering modes on the same backlit scene produce three different exposure decisions — the same camera, same shutter button, three different photos

The exposure meter samples scene luminance and computes the camera setting that would render that luminance as Zone V (mid-grey). It cannot know which subject you care about — different modes encode different assumptions. Spot samples a 1–3° centre dot. Center-weighted averages but biases the centre. Evaluative divides the frame into zones and pattern-matches.

A backlit portrait: bright window behind a dark figure. Each mode samples differently; the resulting exposure shows the figure as mid-grey (spot on face), as a silhouette (evaluative averages window+figure), or in between (center-weighted).
